target.title: fix #78919: [Bug] ACP sessions_spawn doesn't route images to Codex's native vision like acpx codex exec does
target.body:
## Summary
Fixes #78919

`sessions_spawn(runtime="acp", agentId="codex")` now routes validated image attachments through the gateway image attachment contract so they reach the ACP turn path used by Codex native vision.

## Changes
- Convert ACP turn attachments to the gateway-supported nested base64 image source shape before calling the gateway `agent` method.
- Keep ACP runtime turns using `{ mediaType, data }` after gateway parsing, where that shape belongs.
- Add `tools.sessions_spawn.attachments.acpEnabled` so ACP inline image forwarding requires an explicit second opt-in in addition to `attachments.enabled`.
- Preserve existing attachment limits and validation: image-only for ACP, strict base64 handling, duplicate/name checks, max files, per-file bytes, and total bytes.
- Update tests, config schema/types, and gateway attachment docs.

## Changed files
```text
docs/gateway/config-tools.md                 |  10 +-
src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts                 |  39 ++++++++
src/agents/acp-spawn.ts                      |  29 ++++++
src/agents/agent-command.ts                  |   3 +
src/agents/subagent-attachments.ts           | 130 ++++++++++++++++++++++++-
src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts | 136 +++++++++++++++++++++++++--
src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts      |  13 ++-
src/config/types.tools.ts                    |  15 +++
src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts       |   1 +
9 files changed, 361 insertions(+), 15 deletions(-)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** ACP `sessions_spawn` image attachments were rejected before dispatch or could be dropped at the gateway boundary when ACP turn attachment shape was sent as RPC input.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64 host with an isolated Ubuntu jammy Docker rootfs for Codex ACP compatibility, Node v24.16.0, OpenClaw checkout at PR head `4970f72` in `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-78936-proof`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  # Direct module-path proof that the PR shape survives gateway normalization.
  PATH="/home/0668001030/.config/nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin:$PATH" \
    env -C /media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw \
    node --import tsx \
    /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-78919-evidence/acp-image-routing-proof.mjs

  # Live Codex ACP image proof in an isolated PR-head worktree.
  docker run --rm --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
    -v /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-78936-proof:/repo \
    -v /media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw/node_modules:/repo/node_modules \
    -v /home/0668001030/.config/nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0:/node24 \
    -v /usr/local/node:/usr/local/node \
    -v /home/0668001030/.codex:/root/.codex:rw \
    -w /repo \
    -e OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 \
    -e OPENCLAW_LIVE_ACP_BIND=1 \
    -e OPENCLAW_LIVE_ACP_BIND_AGENT=codex \
    -e OPENCLAW_LIVE_ACP_BIND_IMAGE_PROBE=1 \
    openclaw-codex-proof:jammy \
    sh -lc 'node scripts/test-live.mjs -- src/gateway/gateway-acp-bind.live.test.ts'
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:** Local terminal capture from the direct OpenClaw module path:
  ```json
  {
    "resolverStatus": "ok",
    "acpAttachment": {
      "mediaType": "image/png",
      "data": "cG5nLWJ5dGVz"
    },
    "gatewayAttachment": {
      "type": "image",
      "source": {
        "type": "base64",
        "media_type": "image/png",
        "data": "cG5nLWJ5dGVz"
      }
    },
    "normalizedAttachment": {
      "type": "image",
      "mimeType": "image/png",
      "content": "cG5nLWJ5dGVz"
    },
    "droppedByGatewayNormalizer": false
  }
  ```
  Local terminal capture from the live OpenClaw gateway + Codex ACP image probe:
  ```text
  [live-acp-bind] using Codex ACP model gpt-5.5
  [live-acp-bind] starting gateway on port 48000
  [live-acp-bind] gateway startup returned
  [live-acp-bind] gateway port is reachable
  [live-acp-bind] gateway websocket connected
  [live-acp-bind] bind command completed
  [live-acp-bind] binding announced for session agent:codex:acp:d21fe5a2-1982-4ea8-ad73-a722500a77af
  [live-acp-bind] follow-up turn completed (attempt 1)
  [live-acp-bind] memory recall turn completed (attempt 1)
  [live-acp-bind] bound session transcript retained the previous token
  [live-acp-bind] memory marker turn completed (attempt 1)
  [live-acp-bind] bound session transcript contains the final marker token
  [live-acp-bind] image turn completed (attempt 1)
  [live-acp-bind] bound session classified the probe image
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The ACP-specific attachment resolver accepts the image only when both `attachments.enabled` and `attachments.acpEnabled` are true, the dispatch boundary uses the gateway-supported base64 image source shape, gateway attachment normalization preserves the image payload instead of dropping it, and a live OpenClaw gateway routed the probe image through Codex ACP far enough for the bound session to classify the image.
- **What was not tested:** The same live run continued past the successful image probe and later failed in cron CLI verification because this isolated source worktree did not have `dist/entry.mjs` build output for `openclaw.mjs cron list`. That later cron check is unrelated to ACP image delivery. No live Codex output is fabricated here.

## Regression Test Plan

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm test src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts test/scripts/lint-suppressions.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `pnpm check:test-types`

## Root Cause

- `spawnAcpDirect` previously passed ACP turn attachments in `{ mediaType, data }` form to the gateway `agent` RPC boundary, but gateway normalization only preserves attachments with `content` or nested base64 `source` content.
- ACP image forwarding also needed its own explicit opt-in so enabling subagent file materialization does not automatically enable inline ACP image forwarding.

target.title: perf(qmd): persistent export-state cache + stat fast path in exportSessions
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** For agents with thousands of session transcripts (Eddie's main: 4,052 JSONL files / 158 MB), `qmdManager.exportSessions()` was the dominant gateway CPU consumer. Every interval (5 min) and every boot, it walked every session file and called `buildSessionEntry` per file — full JSONL read + parse + redaction + hashing + render — even when nothing had changed.
- **Why it matters:** The existing skip mechanism (`exportedSessionState` in-memory `Map`) gated only the actual Markdown write, not the upstream work; and the `Map` didn't survive restart. Result: every 5-minute interval triggered a full re-walk + re-render cycle, plus a cold start every gateway boot.
- **What changed:**
  1. Persist the per-file state cache to `<qmdDir>/sessions/.export-state.json` alongside a `SESSION_EXPORT_RENDER_VERSION` sentinel and the resolved `exportDir`. Loaded lazily on the first `exportSessions()` call (so it works in both `full` and `status` init modes). Atomic write via `writeFileWithinRoot`. Schema / renderVersion / exportDir mismatch → drop cache cleanly.
  2. Add a stat-based fast path before `buildSessionEntry`. If the source JSONL's `(size, mtimeMs)` match the cached entry, skip `buildSessionEntry` entirely and trust the existing export. The slow path still runs on any stat change and remains responsible for hashing, redacting, and producing the markdown qmd consumes.
- **Scope boundary:** `renderSessionMarkdown`, `redactSensitiveText`, line wrapping, and the markdown qmd consumes are unchanged. `buildSessionEntry` itself is unchanged on the slow path. The persisted cache is per-`exportDir` and invalidates cleanly on `SESSION_EXPORT_RENDER_VERSION` bumps.

**Effect on Eddie's main agent (4,052 sessions / 158 MB):**
- Boot qmd sync: **312s → single-digit seconds**
- Interval qmd sync: **38s → ~50-500 ms**
- The every-5-min CPU spike + GC tail collapses

## Linked issue

- Closes openclaw-ys0 (Beads tracking issue)

## Test coverage

New tests cover correctness of the cache contract:

- Coverage level: Unit test (added two cases to `qmd-manager.test.ts`)
- Target test or file: `extensions/qmd/qmd-manager.test.ts` (`load+persist roundtrip` and `exportDir mismatch invalidation`)
- Scenario the tests lock in:
  1. State cache loads from disk on first call, persists changes, survives a fresh manager construction.
  2. `exportDir` mismatch (for example, a config change) drops the persisted cache and re-derives state.

Existing 102 tests still pass.

## Tradeoffs / known caveats

- A `touch -d <original-mtime>` rewrite of a JSONL would be missed by the stat fast path until another size/mtime-changing event. **Not introduced here** — the previous skip-write-on-hash-match already swallowed same-content rewrites; this PR doesn't make that surface worse.
- **`SESSION_EXPORT_RENDER_VERSION` bump required when changing** `renderSessionMarkdown`, `redactSensitiveText`, line wrapping, or anything else that affects the bytes written to `qmd/sessions/*.md`. Documented inline at the constant declaration.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** The QMD session-export cache must avoid rebuilding unchanged session markdown on repeated syncs, while still rebuilding a session export when the cached markdown target has been deleted but the source JSONL size/mtime still match the persisted `.export-state.json` entry.

**Real environment tested:** Real `QmdMemoryManager` from the topic branch (`perf/qmd-export-stat-fast-path` at `2a92f91478`) with `qmd 2.1.0` available on PATH, driven by a Node/tsx script against an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` under `/tmp`. The driver constructed the production manager and called `manager.sync({ reason: "manual" })`; it did not use Vitest, `vi.mock`, or spawn fakes.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the real-driver command from the topic worktree:

```text
$ ./node_modules/.bin/tsx /tmp/.../qmd-export-driver.mjs
```

The driver executed four phases: first boot export, second boot with unchanged inputs, manual deletion of `session-1.md` while `.export-state.json` survived, then third boot to prove the missing cached target is rebuilt.

**Evidence after fix:** Copied live output from the real-driver run in PR comment 4384871421:

```text
=== Phase 1: first boot — expect markdown written ===
-- boot 1 --
[memory] qmd sync completed for agent "main" reason=manual durationMs=429
  sync done in 445 ms
export file size: 49 bytes; contains 'hello tank-real-proof': true
export mtimeMs: 1778038392470.7026
cache version: 1  entries: 1  target = undefined

=== Phase 2: second boot, same inputs — expect FAST PATH (no rewrite) ===
-- boot 2 --
[memory] qmd sync completed for agent "main" reason=manual durationMs=313
  sync done in 315 ms
export mtimeMs after boot 2: 1778038392470.7026  (== boot 1 mtime? true )

=== Phase 3: delete session-1.md while .export-state.json cache survives ===
session-1.md exists? false  (cache file still here: true )

=== Phase 4: third boot — fast-path access check must fall through and rebuild ===
-- boot 3 --
[memory] qmd sync completed for agent "main" reason=manual durationMs=384
  sync done in 387 ms
rebuilt export size: 49 bytes; contains 'hello tank-real-proof': true
rebuilt mtimeMs: 1778038393222.7556  (> boot 1 mtime? true )
```

**Observed result after fix:** The second boot preserved the exact markdown `mtimeMs`, proving the unchanged-input fast path skipped the rewrite. After deleting the markdown export, the third boot rebuilt it and restored the expected content (`hello tank-real-proof`) with a newer `mtimeMs`, proving the missing-target regression path falls through to the slow rebuild instead of silently trusting stale cache state.

**What was not tested:** A thousands-session timing benchmark on Eddie's full 4,052-session corpus was not rerun for this proof. The live driver proves the behavioral cache invariants; the original timing numbers remain observational context from the production-sized main-agent workload.

target.title: perf(models-config): targetProvider short-circuit with disk-vs-config validation
target.body:
## Summary

Splits the targetProvider short-circuit half of #72869 into a standalone PR.

Adds a fast path to `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` for callers that know which provider/model they are about to use (e.g. the pi-embedded runner). When set, the implicit-provider-discovery pipeline can be skipped IF the on-disk `models.json` provider entry **structurally matches** what the current configuration would produce.

## Why structural comparison

A naive "is configured" check (does any non-empty credential exist on disk for this provider) silently bypasses several real failure modes:

- **Rotated apiKey**: cold start with new key in config, old key still on disk, presence-only check fires, all calls fail with 401 until something else invalidates.
- **Attacker-tampered baseUrl**: redirect to exfil endpoint kept across restarts.
- **Attacker-injected headers**: arbitrary auth material kept across restarts.

The new `readExistingProviderMatchesConfig` compares all four security-relevant fields:

| Field | Comparison |
|---|---|
| `apiKey` | resolved through `resolveSecretInputRef` (env-ref expansion via `createConfigRuntimeEnv`) before string equality vs. disk |
| `baseUrl` | exact string equality |
| `headers` | stable structural equality (key-order independent) |
| `auth` | stable structural equality |

Any mismatch falls through to full planning + write. This is safe to use on cold start and after gateway restart.

## API

Adds `EnsureOpenClawModelsJsonOptions` exported type with `targetProvider` and `targetModel`, layered on top of the existing options shape (kept all of `pluginMetadataSnapshot`, `workspaceDir`, `providerDiscoveryProviderIds`, `providerDiscoveryTimeoutMs`).

## Tests

6 cases in `models-config.target-provider-short-circuit.test.ts`:
- `hit-on-match` — full structural match short-circuits planning
- `miss-on-rotated-key` — config `apiKey` change forces a full plan
- `miss-on-baseUrl-change` — tampered disk `baseUrl` rejects short-circuit
- `miss-on-tampered-headers` — any disk header drift rejects short-circuit
- `miss-on-cold-cache` — empty in-memory cache + missing disk file forces a plan
- `hit-after-warm-fingerprint` — warm in-memory cache reuse

## Dependencies

This is one of two PRs split out of the original combined #72869. The cache-fingerprint work (auth-profiles content hash, models.json drift detection, fingerprint hashing, security hardening) is in #73260.

This branch can land independently of the cache PR; the two changes touch different code paths.

## Related

- Splits out of #72869 (short-circuit half)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Target-provider models-config short-circuit safety from PR #73261, including provider-level `api` drift and uncacheable models hash fail-closed behavior.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw topic branch `perf/models-config-target-provider-short-circuit` at `2f89c7b99a`, including final #73260 ContentHashOutcome contract, isolated temporary agent directory, production `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` invoked through a `tsx` runtime driver with no test harness or mocks.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran a real proof driver after the patch covering steady-state targetProvider hit, provider `api` drift, and oversize `models.json`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Full copied runtime output is in the PR comment: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/73261#issuecomment-4384975648

  Excerpt of copied live output:
  ```text
  ensureOpenClawModelsJson targetProvider=... steady-state: wrote=false
  provider api changed on disk/config comparison: short-circuit refused; full plan wrote=true
  planner restored config-side provider api
  models.json > 1 MiB: scoped cache refused; full plan wrote=true
  planner shrank models.json below 1 MiB
  proof: PASS
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: The production path keeps the short-circuit only for structurally matching provider config, refuses provider-level `api` drift, and refuses uncacheable models hashes instead of treating them as stable cache hits.
- **What was not tested**: Nothing else for this PR's changed behavior beyond the isolated local runtime proof and supplemental automated validation.

target.title: perf(plugins): thread prepared manifestPlugins through runtime model-id normalize chain
target.body:
## Summary

Extends the prepared-manifest-metadata threading from #82814 into the runtime plugin-normalize branch so callers that already carry prepared `manifestPlugins` skip the residual `installs.json` slot-or-load disk walk in `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`. Closes the gap that #82814's measurements left visible (Kova `agent-cold-warm-message` was at 28 metadata scans per cold+warm sample post-#82814).

Builds on the architecture shakkernerd endorsed when closing #78589: carrying prepared facts through the hot consumers rather than adding another lazy slot-refill path. Does not touch `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` itself.

## Why

`#82814` (`fix: reuse plugin manifest metadata safely`, merged 2026-05-17) threaded prepared `manifestPlugins` through the static model-id normalize path (`normalizeStaticProviderModelId` in `src/agents/model-ref-shared.ts`) so consumers stop consulting the `current-plugin-metadata-snapshot` single-slot handoff in `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`. That eliminated most of the hot-path `installs.json` disk walks: Kova metadata scans dropped 394 → 28 per cold+warm sample (~93% reduction).

The remaining 28 scans come from the runtime plugin-normalize branch. `src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts` already receives `options.manifestPlugins` from its caller and threads it into the static branch (line 51-54); the runtime branch immediately below (line 58-66) silently drops it, and the chain falls through to the slot-or-load disk walk:

\`\`\`
src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts:59
  → normalizeProviderModelIdWithRuntime(...)         (no plugins param)
    → normalizeProviderModelIdWithPlugin(params)     (no plugins param)
      → normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest(params) (params.plugins undefined)
        → loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies   (slot-or-load disk walk)
\`\`\`

`normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest` already honors `params.plugins` to short-circuit:

\`\`\`ts
function loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies(params) {
  if (params.plugins) {
    return collectManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies(params.plugins);  // prepared path
  }
  const { snapshot, cacheable } = resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies(params);  // slot-or-load
  // ...
}
\`\`\`

The intermediate functions just never accepted a `plugins` field. This PR adds it as an additive optional param and forwards `options?.manifestPlugins` from the existing call site that already has it in scope.

## How

Three surgical edits:

1. **`src/agents/provider-model-normalization.runtime.ts`** — add optional `plugins?: readonly Pick<PluginManifestRecord, \"modelIdNormalization\">[]` to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithRuntime`'s params. The body just forwards `params` unchanged to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithPlugin`, so no behavioral change beyond the type signature.

2. **`src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts`** — add the same optional `plugins?` field to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithPlugin`'s params. The existing `normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest(params)` call already forwards everything, so `plugins` flows straight through to where it's honored.

3. **`src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts:59`** — at the runtime call site, thread `options?.manifestPlugins` into the runtime call. Spread-conditional so the call shape is unchanged when `manifestPlugins` is absent.

Net diff: +6 LOC across three source files (plus tests and changelog).

## Relationship to the previously closed PR #78589

`#78589` proposed a different fix at a different layer: refilling the `current-plugin-metadata-snapshot` slot after a cold load inside `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`. shakkernerd closed it 2026-05-17 as superseded by #82814 with the framing: *current `main` now avoids the release-blocking path by carrying prepared manifest/plugin metadata through the hot model/runtime/session consumers rather than relying on another lazy republish point* — and invited follow-up: *If there is still a distinct empty-slot case after current main, please reopen with current-main repro steps, the exact SHA, and trace/timing evidence for the remaining path.*

This PR is that follow-up, scoped to the residual gap with a fix that **extends the consumer-threading pattern shakkernerd endorsed** rather than reintroducing the slot-refill approach. The original `archive/perf-manifest-model-id-lazy-publish` git tag in `zeroaltitude/openclaw` preserves the prior branch for reference.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Callers that pass prepared `manifestPlugins` to `normalizeModelRef` (via `ModelManifestNormalizationContext`) had that metadata silently dropped when the runtime plugin-normalize branch was taken, causing the chain to fall through to the slot-or-load disk walk in `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` even though prepared metadata was available in the caller's scope.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw worktree against `origin/main` at `03125c8e13` (synced from `upstream/main`), branch `perf/manifest-model-id-thread-runtime-plugins`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-selection.plugin-runtime.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-manifest-workspace.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-model-id-normalization.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/provider-model-normalization.runtime.ts src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts src/agents/model-selection.plugin-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: tsgo:core exit 0; tsgo:core:test exit 0; vitest 9 files / 349 tests passed; oxlint exit 0.
- **Observed result after fix**: With prepared `manifestPlugins`, the runtime mock receives `{ plugins: [...] }` and the production chain hands them through to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest`, where the existing `params.plugins` short-circuit in `loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies` takes the prepared path and skips `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` entirely. Without prepared plugins, the runtime call shape is byte-identical to current `main` (regression test pins this).
- **What was not tested**: I have not run the full Kova `agent-cold-warm-message` repro on a deployed-style mock provider environment to measure the 28 → ~3 scan reduction in production scale. The mechanism is identical to #82814's: when `params.plugins` is present, `loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies` returns immediately from `collectManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies(params.plugins)` without invoking `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`, so the savings scale 1:1 with how often the runtime plugin-normalize branch is hit on cold-slot turns. Maintainers with the Kova harness can re-run that scenario; happy to add that measurement if you can point me at the canonical fixture.

## Tests

Added in `src/agents/model-selection.plugin-runtime.test.ts`:

- `forwards manifestPlugins to the runtime normalization call so it can skip the slot-or-load disk walk` — asserts that when `normalizeModelRef(..., { manifestPlugins: prepared })` is called, the runtime mock receives `{ plugins: prepared, ... }`.
- `omits plugins from the runtime call when no manifestPlugins are prepared (preserves current behavior)` — asserts the call shape is unchanged for the no-prepared-metadata path.

All existing test cases in the affected files continue to pass.

## Verification

- `tsgo:core`: clean
- `tsgo:core:test`: clean
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs <5 affected files>`: 9 files / 349 tests passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <4 affected source files>`: 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `git diff --check`: clean
- Branched from `origin/main` (= `upstream/main` at `03125c8e13`); no rebase needed at submission.

target.title: feat(claude): add claude-bridge app-server harness extension
target.body:
## Summary

Inspiration: https://openclaw.ai/blog/openai-models-in-openclaw-done-right; achieve parity for Claude

OpenClaw currently has no first-class harness for Anthropic models. Anthropic users either fall back to a generic provider client (no native tool execution, no extended-thinking awareness, no per-turn rate-limit surfacing) or run a Claude harness out-of-tree with no contract for OpenClaw's plugin-SDK seams.

The codex harness already ships in-tree with the same shape we want — `extensions/codex/src/app-server/` is a codex-app-server JSON-RPC client over stdio. We have a working Anthropic-side equivalent built and running in production on the author's setup; opening it for maintainer steer before doing more SDK work on top.

**What is the intended outcome?**

- Bundled extension `@openclaw/claude` registers `claude-bridge` as a harness option in the model picker.
- At turn-start, the extension spawns an `openclaw-claude-bridge` subprocess and speaks the codex-shaped JSON-RPC protocol over stdio. The server (currently published as `@zeroaltitude/openclaw-claude-bridge` — fork-preview path; would migrate to `@openclaw/openclaw-claude-bridge` if the maintainers republish it) handles Anthropic API I/O, rate-limit surfacing, image-payload sanitization, and plugin-thread-config persistence.
- Bridge-side handles event projection into OpenClaw's notification/accumulator surface, transcript mirroring with idempotency keys, dynamic-tool aggregate output cap, vision-tools de-duplication, and `/claude` slash commands.
- See `extensions/claude/README.md` for the full feature map.

**What is intentionally out of scope?**

- Republishing the server under `@openclaw/openclaw-claude-bridge` — needs maintainer decision; the `@zeroaltitude` scope dependency is the current placeholder.
- Migrating to OpenClaw's standard plugin-SDK provider hooks beyond what `extensions/codex/` already uses. The bridge follows codex's pattern verbatim where applicable.
- Anything outside the harness bridge surface.

**What does success look like?** Anthropic users get parity with codex users: native tool dispatch, rate-limit-aware errors, extended-thinking-friendly transcript mirroring, harness-level event projection. No regressions to existing harnesses.

**What should reviewers focus on?**

1. **Scope concern — am I taking too much core?** The branch touches `src/plugins/command-registration.ts`, `src/plugins/current-plugin-metadata-snapshot.ts`, `src/plugins/agent-tool-result-middleware-types.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.ts`, `src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.ts`, and `src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts`. Each is small and necessary to wire a harness extension, but the contribution guide says "one thing per PR." Happy to split into prep PRs first if maintainers prefer.
2. **External-plugin vs. bundled-extension** — Per CONTRIBUTING.md "most features should be third-party plugins." Open to packaging this entirely as an external plugin instead of `extensions/claude/`, if that is preferred.
3. **Anthropic-API behavior delegated to a separate server repo** — Most of the actual API logic lives in the server (https://github.com/zeroaltitude/openclaw-plugins/tree/main/openclaw-claude/server). Reviewers will probably want to inspect that companion repo before approving.
4. **`claude-bridge` harness id naming** — there's no precedent for "<vendor>-bridge" harness names; renamable. (The reason I did this initially was to ensure that I wasn't representing that this work was by anthropic -- matters more on the server naming than the in-openclaw extension tho)

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Anthropic-model agents previously had no in-tree harness; the bridge unlocks native tool runs against Claude through the same JSON-RPC pattern codex uses.
- **Real environment tested:** Author's openclaw gateway running on Linux (WSL2 / Ubuntu), Node 22.x. Author's agents  are configured with `claude-bridge` as their harness and are in daily active use against `claude-sonnet-4-6` and `claude-opus-4-7`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. `pnpm install && pnpm build`
  2. `openclaw gateway restart --deep`
  3. Send a Slack DM to one of the claude-bridge agents
  4. Agent responds with native Anthropic tools used; transcript file contains the projected events.
  5. `/claude status` in the same thread prints shared-client liveness.
- **Evidence after fix:** Daily-driver production use (multiple Slack threads/turns per day) over the past ~2 weeks on the author's setup. Tank (a codex-harness agent) and Tabitha (claude-bridge) work side-by-side on the same gateway. Transcript files at `~/.openclaw/agents/<id>/transcripts/` show the projected event stream. No fresh standalone capture for this PR; proof is the ongoing production usage.
- **Observed result after fix:** Anthropic turns complete normally; tool calls fire; rate-limit messages are user-actionable; resume across restart works via the `.claude-binding.json` sidecar.
- **What was not tested:**
  - Windows/macOS gateway hosts (only Linux/WSL2 verified).
  - Bun runtime (Node 22 only).
  - Anthropic enterprise/Bedrock/Vertex API surfaces (only public Anthropic API).
  - Concurrent multi-account scenarios beyond 3 agents.
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** Author runs a single-host gateway. The server is currently published under the author's `@zeroaltitude` npm scope (fork-preview), so reproducing this requires installing the bridge from that scope until maintainers republish.
- **Before evidence:** N/A — no prior in-tree harness for Anthropic to compare against.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm build` ✓ (local)
- `pnpm test extensions/claude` ✓ (local — extension unit tests, including event projector, dynamic-tools middleware port, thread fork policy envelope, transcript mirror dedupe)
- `pnpm test:extensions` not run since last rebase — recommend Testbox/Crabbox run before non-draft review.
- No `CHANGELOG.md` edit per contributor rule; maintainer to add on land.

## Risk checklist

- **Did user-visible behavior change?** Yes — new `claude-bridge` harness appears in model picker; new `/claude` slash command surface.
- **Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** Yes — new plugin `configSchema` (see `extensions/claude/openclaw.plugin.json`). Existing agent configs unaffected unless they explicitly opt into `claude-bridge` harness. Doctor contract added (`extensions/claude/doctor-contract-api.ts`).
- **Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** Yes (intentional). The bridge spawns a subprocess that holds the Anthropic API key in-memory and makes network calls to Anthropic. Subprocess lifecycle is owned by the harness, not user shell. The plugin SDK enabler changes (e.g. `command-registration.ts`) are additive and follow existing codex-extension precedent.
- **Highest-risk area:** Server-bridge stdio lifecycle and the few SDK enabler edits in `src/plugins/**`. If the SDK changes accidentally relax a guard for non-claude paths, every harness would inherit the change.
- **How is that risk mitigated?** The SDK changes are narrowly scoped — each follows a codex-extension-shaped precedent. Happy to factor them into a prep PR if reviewers want to land them separately first.

## Current review state

- **Next action:** Maintainer steer on scope: (a) accept as bundled extension as-is, (b) split SDK enablers into a prep PR first, or (c) request packaging as an external plugin.
- **Still waiting on:** Maintainer decision on the above. CI run on this push.
- **Bot/reviewer comments addressed:** None yet — opening draft.

---

🤖 **AI-assisted disclosure:** Developed with the assistance of Claude Code (anthropic.com/claude-code). All design decisions, production deployment, and real-behavior verification are human-driven; AI assistance was used for implementation across multiple sessions. Session transcripts available on request.

target.title: fix(cli): honor ZDOTDIR/XDG_CONFIG_HOME in completion install (#63069)
target.body:
## Summary

Shell completion install/check helpers hardcoded zsh, fish, and bash profile paths under `$HOME`, ignoring `$ZDOTDIR` and `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` and disagreeing on bash `.bashrc` vs `.bash_profile`. Reporter on macOS / zsh with `$ZDOTDIR=$HOME/.config/zsh` saw completion silently written to `$HOME/.zshrc` (a file the shell never reads), so completion never activates in new shells. This PR centralizes profile-path resolution behind a single resolver that honors both env vars, treats `.bashrc`/`.bash_profile` as a priority-ordered candidate list, and lets the check and install paths agree on the same file.

## Root Cause

- `src/cli/completion-runtime.ts:129` (before): `getShellProfilePath()` returned `path.join(home, ".zshrc")`, `path.join(home, ".bashrc")`, `path.join(home, ".config", "fish", "config.fish")` regardless of `$ZDOTDIR` / `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME`.
- `src/cli/completion-runtime.ts:151` (before): `isCompletionInstalled()` called `getShellProfilePath(shell)` (HOME-only) and returned `false` for bash even when the install actually wrote to `.bash_profile`.
- `src/cli/completion-runtime.ts:215` (before): `installCompletion()` separately hardcoded `path.join(home, ".zshrc")`, `path.join(home, ".bashrc")` / `.bash_profile` fallback, and `path.join(home, ".config", "fish", "config.fish")` — so when `.bashrc` was absent, install wrote to `.bash_profile` while check still looked at `.bashrc`, causing repeated `Enable completion?` prompts on every `openclaw update`.
- Execution path: `setup` / `update` / `completion --install` → `installCompletion()` writes to one path → `isCompletionInstalled()` (next session) checks the OTHER path → both report `false` so install is offered again, and the previously written source line lives in a file the shell never reads.
- clawsweeper review on #63069: *"Queue a focused fix PR because the report is valid, the affected files and tests are clear, and the target-repo implementation PR is closed unmerged."* and *"A shared resolver used by both status and install paths is the narrowest maintainable fix."*

## Changes

- `src/cli/completion-runtime.ts`: introduce `resolveShellProfilePathCandidates(shell, env, homeDir)` that returns candidate paths in priority order — `$ZDOTDIR/.zshrc` then `$HOME/.zshrc` for zsh, `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/fish/config.fish` then `$HOME/.config/fish/config.fish` for fish, `.bashrc` then `.bash_profile` for bash. Make `getShellProfilePath()` async, return the first existing candidate (or the first candidate as the canonical install target when none exist). Update `isCompletionInstalled` and `usesSlowDynamicCompletion` to `await` it. Drop the duplicate hardcoded paths in `installCompletion` and route entirely through `getShellProfilePath`, so check and install always agree on the same file.
- `src/cli/completion-runtime.profile-path.test.ts` (new): 12 colocated regression tests covering `$ZDOTDIR` zsh priority, `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` fish priority, bash `.bashrc → .bash_profile` order, whitespace-only `$ZDOTDIR` handling, `os.homedir()` fallback when `env.HOME` is unset, and live `isCompletionInstalled()` against real temp directories where the OpenClaw marker lives in the non-default file.

Net diff: **+237 / −36 LOC across 2 files** (1 new test file + 1 production file).

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: macOS / zsh users with `$ZDOTDIR=$HOME/.config/zsh` (a common dotfile layout, often via `/etc/zshenv` or `.zshenv`) see `openclaw completion --install` write the source line to `$HOME/.zshrc` instead of `$ZDOTDIR/.zshrc`, so completion never loads in their shell. Same class of bug applies to fish users with `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` set, and to bash users who only have `~/.bash_profile` (macOS default): `isCompletionInstalled()` checks `.bashrc` while `installCompletion()` falls back to `.bash_profile`, so every `openclaw update` re-asks `Enable completion?`. Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/63069
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-65656` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/cli/completion-runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, slices the patched `resolveShellProfilePathCandidates` + `getShellProfilePath` block (offset-pinned), prints its SHA-256, then runs both functions against (a) synthetic env tables matching the reporter scenarios and (b) real temp directories on disk where the OpenClaw marker is dropped into the non-default file. No mocks. PR head: `5c935bf8` on `MoerAI:fix/shell-completion-xdg-zdotdir`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git checkout fix/shell-completion-xdg-zdotdir && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr63069.mts`. The smoke uses `fs.mkdtemp` to build a real `$ZDOTDIR/.zshrc` and `$HOME/.zshrc` pair, drops the `# OpenClaw Completion` marker into the `$ZDOTDIR` one only, and asks `getShellProfilePath("zsh", {HOME, ZDOTDIR})` which file it picks. Same for the macOS bash case where only `.bash_profile` exists.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `5c935bf8` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, patched resolver block 1961 bytes, SHA-256 `6d240bd88d09e8184ee26f26f4b7336d5a42940ecba6ce63e00e7fd65a31d3e6`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr63069.mts
=== Patched profile-path resolver — bytes from src/cli/completion-runtime.ts ===
offset: 4838 -> 6799 ( 1961 bytes)
SHA-256: 6d240bd88d09e8184ee26f26f4b7336d5a42940ecba6ce63e00e7fd65a31d3e6

=== Scenario A: ZDOTDIR set, $ZDOTDIR/.zshrc preferred ===
candidates: [ '\\home\\example\\.config\\zsh\\.zshrc', '\\home\\example\\.zshrc' ]

=== Scenario B: ZDOTDIR unset, falls back to $HOME/.zshrc ===
candidates: [ '\\home\\example\\.zshrc' ]

=== Scenario C: bash .bashrc + .bash_profile (macOS fallback) ===
candidates: [ '\\home\\example\\.bashrc', '\\home\\example\\.bash_profile' ]

=== Scenario D: XDG_CONFIG_HOME set, fish preferred ===
candidates: [
  '\\home\\example\\.alt-config\\fish\\config.fish',
  '\\home\\example\\.config\\fish\\config.fish'
]

=== Scenario E: getShellProfilePath picks the first EXISTING candidate (ZDOTDIR wins) ===
zdotdir zshrc exists: true
home zshrc exists: true
getShellProfilePath returned: C:\Users\pss\AppData\Local\Temp\openclaw-pr63069-VIFFw8\.config\zsh\.zshrc
matches zdotdir candidate: true

=== Scenario F: macOS bash with only .bash_profile (no .bashrc) ===
.bashrc exists: false
.bash_profile exists: true
getShellProfilePath returned: C:\Users\pss\AppData\Local\Temp\openclaw-pr63069-bash-Aryipc\.bash_profile
matches .bash_profile: true

=== Pass/fail summary ===
  PASS  ZDOTDIR pref
  PASS  ZDOTDIR fallback
  PASS  no ZDOTDIR -> .zshrc
  PASS  bash .bashrc first
  PASS  bash .bash_profile 2nd
  PASS  fish XDG pref
  PASS  fish fallback
  PASS  picks existing zdotdir zshrc
  PASS  macOS picks .bash_profile w/o .bashrc
~~~

- Observed result after fix: The resolver returns `$ZDOTDIR/.zshrc` first when `ZDOTDIR` is set (Scenario A), `$HOME/.zshrc` when it is not (Scenario B), `.bashrc` then `.bash_profile` for bash (Scenario C), and `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/fish/config.fish` first when set (Scenario D). The live filesystem tests confirm `getShellProfilePath("zsh", {HOME, ZDOTDIR})` actually picks the existing `$ZDOTDIR` file when both candidates exist on disk (Scenario E, this directly satisfies the reporter's macOS / zsh / `$ZDOTDIR=$HOME/.config/zsh` setup), and falls through to `.bash_profile` on a macOS-style bash setup where only that file exists (Scenario F, this satisfies the bash `.bashrc`/`.bash_profile` check/install agreement bug). All 9 assertions PASS against the patched resolver pinned by SHA-256 `6d240bd88d09e8184ee26f26f4b7336d5a42940ecba6ce63e00e7fd65a31d3e6`.
- What was not tested: An interactive `openclaw onboard` / `openclaw update` flow was not exercised on the contributor machine. The fix is a pure path-resolution change inside the existing check/install helpers; the resolver returns plain strings that flow into `fs.readFile` / `fs.writeFile` unchanged, so the only behavioral change is the file path picked. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live macOS interactive smoke is required; the reporter's issue already contains a concrete `$ZDOTDIR=~/.config/zsh` reproducer.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/cli/completion-runtime.profile-path.test.ts` — 12 new colocated regression cases (ZDOTDIR pref/fallback, whitespace-only ZDOTDIR, XDG fish pref/fallback, bash candidate order, os.homedir() fallback, plus 4 live-tempdir `isCompletionInstalled` cases covering ZDOTDIR priority, `.bash_profile`-only macOS, `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` fish, and the no-profile false case).
- `pnpm test src/cli/completion-cli.test.ts src/cli/completion-cli.write-state.test.ts src/wizard/setup.completion.test.ts` — unchanged, exercise the broader completion CLI; the resolver refactor preserves the original behavior when neither `ZDOTDIR` nor `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set and `.bashrc` exists, so existing tests continue to pass.

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix for #63069. `installCompletion()` no longer duplicates the per-shell path logic; both the check path (`isCompletionInstalled`, `usesSlowDynamicCompletion`) and the install path now share `getShellProfilePath()`, so `.bashrc` vs `.bash_profile` and `$ZDOTDIR`/`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` stay consistent.
- Compatibility: when neither env var is set and `.bashrc` exists, the resolver returns exactly the same paths as the previous implementation, so existing installs are unaffected. Powershell paths are unchanged.
- Out of scope: `src/commands/doctor-completion.ts:111` and `:161` print hardcoded reload-hint strings (`source ~/.zshrc` etc.). Those are info-only messages on the success path and not on the failing `isCompletionInstalled` / `installCompletion` arithmetic; a separate cosmetic follow-up can route them through the same resolver.
- This follows the conservative direction in clawsweeper's review on #63069: *"A shared resolver used by both status and install paths is the narrowest maintainable fix; the closed target PR and cross-repo fork PR are useful source context, but the target repository still needs the change on main."*

Closes #63069

target.title: fix(gateway): include MCP tools in effective inventory
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the Agent Tools dashboard / `tools.effective` inventory originally reported only static OpenClaw tools, so configured bundled MCP tools could be usable at runtime but absent from the session-effective inventory.
- Solution: split the Gateway surface so `tools.effective` is a read-only peek projection with explicit MCP notices, and add `tools.effective.refresh` as an `operator.write` method that performs live MCP discovery/materialization on demand.
- What changed: effective inventory projection supports `source: "mcp"`; session MCP runtimes expose peek-only catalog metadata; the Gateway read path no longer cold-starts MCP transports; the refresh path materializes MCP tools through the existing runtime/materializer/final-policy path; Control UI adds an explicit **Refresh Available Tools** action; SDK/docs/protocol/tests cover the new split.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no provider transport changes, no Tool Search/Code Mode raw provider-body changes, no auto-reply `/tools` command changes, no install-package-dir changes, no broad shared-runtime refactor, and no claim that #80909 or the reporter-specific SSE raw provider-body path is fixed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #: N/A (intentionally not closing #76724; this keeps the reporter-specific SSE/runtime-provider-body path out of scope)
- Related / Refs #76724
- Related #70876
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

#76724 reports MCP handshake success while the Agent Tools dashboard stays stuck at the pre-MCP tool count and does not list MCP tools. Runtime code can materialize bundled MCP tools for model runs, but the dashboard inventory used a separate static inventory path. The initial patch made `tools.effective` perform live MCP materialization; after review of the read/security boundary, this update keeps `tools.effective` read-only and moves live discovery behind an explicit refresh method.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `tools.effective` should not cold-start/list MCP transports from a read-scoped dashboard load, while the dashboard must still have an explicit path to discover configured MCP tools and then show the warm catalog.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw Gateway from this branch at `3c7a4e67b6`, QA mock OpenAI provider, real stdio MCP server created by the proof harness, Tool Search disabled, Code Mode disabled.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_REPO_ROOT=/root/repos/openclaw-mcp-tools-effective \
OPENCLAW_LOCAL_HEAD=3c7a4e67b6 \
OPENCLAW_UPSTREAM_COMMIT=110042d840 \
OPENCLAW_RPC_PROOF_SUMMARY=/tmp/openclaw-mcp-tools-effective-d-proof-summary.json \
node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-mcp-tools-effective-d-proof.ts
```

- Evidence after fix:

Copied live Gateway RPC harness output from this branch:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "proof": "D read/refresh split: cold read peek-only, explicit refresh materializes MCP, warm read peeks catalog",
  "upstreamCommit": "110042d840",
  "localHead": "3c7a4e67b6",
  "cold": {
    "noticeIds": ["mcp-not-yet-connected"],
    "containsMcpTarget": false,
    "runtimeCreated": false,
    "groupSummaries": [
      { "id": "core", "source": "core", "toolCount": 18 },
      { "id": "plugin", "source": "plugin", "toolCount": 2 }
    ]
  },
  "refreshed": {
    "noticeIds": [],
    "containsMcpTarget": true,
    "matchingIds": ["reproProbe__probe_tool"],
    "runtimeCreated": true,
    "catalogToolNames": ["probe_tool"],
    "groupSummaries": [
      { "id": "core", "source": "core", "toolCount": 18 },
      { "id": "plugin", "source": "plugin", "toolCount": 2 },
      { "id": "mcp", "source": "mcp", "toolCount": 1, "sampleIds": ["reproProbe__probe_tool"] }
    ]
  },
  "warm": {
    "noticeIds": [],
    "containsMcpTarget": true,
    "matchingIds": ["reproProbe__probe_tool"],
    "groupSummaries": [
      { "id": "core", "source": "core", "toolCount": 18 },
      { "id": "plugin", "source": "plugin", "toolCount": 2 },
      { "id": "mcp", "source": "mcp", "toolCount": 1, "sampleIds": ["reproProbe__probe_tool"] }
    ]
  }
}
```

Additional Control UI browser proof from current head `b8bcb372b3d08b2b193d894b67d486cd9fc376fc`:

```text
Cold Control UI load:
- Panel shows: "MCP servers are configured but not connected yet. Use Refresh Available Tools to discover configured MCP tools."
- `Probe Tool` is absent from Available Right Now.
- Live count is 0.

After clicking "Refresh Available Tools":
- Browser RPC request observed: tools.effective.refresh with {agentId: "main", sessionKey: "agent:main:main"}.
- Available Right Now lists `Probe Tool` with source `MCP`.
- Live count is 1.
```

Screenshots are hosted on a separate fork artifact branch, not committed to this PR diff.

<details open>
<summary>Cold read: configured-MCP notice visible; Probe Tool absent</summary>

![Cold Control UI MCP notice](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nxmxbbd/openclaw/pr-84096-ui-proof/01-cold-mcp-notice.png)
</details>

<details open>
<summary>After refresh: Probe Tool appears as an MCP tool</summary>

![After refresh MCP tool](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nxmxbbd/openclaw/pr-84096-ui-proof/02-after-refresh-mcp-tool.png)
</details>

- Observed result after fix: a cold `tools.effective` read returns only core/plugin groups plus `mcp-not-yet-connected` and does not create a session MCP runtime; explicit `tools.effective.refresh` materializes the configured stdio MCP server and returns `reproProbe__probe_tool`; the next `tools.effective` read shows the warm MCP group via peek without another cold materialization.
- What was not tested: the reporter's original Flask SSE server and release version `v2026.5.2`; Tool Search / Code Mode raw provider-body behavior; #80909; full cross-platform/browser E2E. This PR is scoped to the Gateway/dashboard effective-inventory boundary.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): pre-patch proof on current main returned 20 tools and omitted `reproProbe__probe_tool` from `tools.effective` while the runtime provider path could include it.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `tools.effective` called the static `resolveEffectiveToolInventory()` resolver, which builds from `createOpenClawCodingTools()` only. Bundled MCP tools are appended later in the model-run path through the session MCP runtime/materializer, so the dashboard inventory and runtime provider path diverged.
- Boundary correction in this update: live MCP discovery/listing can start configured stdio/remote transports, so it now lives behind explicit `operator.write` `tools.effective.refresh`; the read-scoped method only peeks at an existing matching-fingerprint runtime catalog and otherwise returns a notice.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered core/plugin/channel grouping but did not cover bundled MCP catalog projection, cold-read side effects, refresh materialization, or stale MCP config fingerprints through the `tools.effective` handler/RPC path.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end-style Gateway RPC proof
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/tools-effective.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts`
  - `packages/sdk/src/index.test.ts`
  - `packages/sdk/src/index.e2e.test.ts`
  - `ui/src/ui/controllers/agents.test.ts`
  - `ui/src/ui/views/agents-panels-tools-skills.browser.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: cold `tools.effective` does not create/list MCP and returns notices; warm read projects an existing same-fingerprint catalog; fingerprint mismatch returns refresh-needed notice instead of stale MCP tools; explicit `tools.effective.refresh` materializes MCP tools and applies final effective policy; UI uses read on normal load and refresh on explicit action.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug and security-boundary concern live at the seam between static inventory, session MCP runtime, Gateway method scope, SDK, and UI refresh behavior.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none found for bundled MCP in `tools-effective` / `tools-effective-inventory` before this patch.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

The Agent Tools panel now loads without implicitly starting configured MCP transports. If MCP servers are configured but not connected/listed yet, the panel shows an explanatory notice and a **Refresh Available Tools** action. Triggering refresh performs live discovery and lets subsequent reads show the warm MCP tools.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before this PR:
[tools.effective] -> [static createOpenClawCodingTools inventory] -> [core/plugin/channel only]

Updated design:
[tools.effective]         -> [static inventory + peek existing MCP catalog] -> [core/plugin/channel + notices or warm mcp]
[tools.effective.refresh] -> [session MCP runtime materialize] -> [final bundled-tool policy] -> [core/plugin/channel + mcp]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No new capability beyond existing configured MCP materialization; yes, a new explicit Gateway method is added.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? Yes, only from explicit `tools.effective.refresh` or existing run-lifecycle MCP materialization, not from cold `tools.effective` reads.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes, refresh can start/list configured MCP transports; it does not invoke MCP tools.
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: live MCP listing can start a configured stdio server or connect to a configured remote endpoint, so this update moves that side effect out of `operator.read` and into explicit `operator.write` `tools.effective.refresh`. The read path only peeks an existing runtime catalog with a matching config fingerprint and returns notices otherwise. Refresh reuses the existing session MCP runtime/materializer/final effective policy path and releases the materializer lease.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local dev environment
- Runtime/container: local Node via `node --import tsx`
- Model/provider: QA mock OpenAI provider, `mock-openai/gpt-5.5`
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway WebSocket RPC `sessions.create`, `tools.effective`, `tools.effective.refresh`, then `tools.effective`
- Relevant config (redacted): `tools.profile: "coding"`, `tools.toolSearch: false`, `tools.codeMode.enabled: false`, `mcp.servers.reproProbe` stdio server command `node <tmp>/dummy-mcp-server.mjs`

### Steps

1. Start QA mock provider and local Gateway from this branch.
2. Create a session with `sessions.create`.
3. Call cold `tools.effective` and assert no MCP runtime was created and `reproProbe__probe_tool` is absent.
4. Call `tools.effective.refresh` and assert `reproProbe__probe_tool` appears in an `mcp` source group.
5. Call warm `tools.effective` and assert the MCP group is projected via peek.

### Expected

- Cold read: no MCP cold-start/listing; notice returned.
- Refresh: MCP listed/materialized through explicit write-scoped method.
- Warm read: MCP group visible through existing catalog peek.

### Actual

- Cold read: `runtimeCreated: false`, `containsMcpTarget: false`, `noticeIds: ["mcp-not-yet-connected"]`.
- Refresh: `runtimeCreated: true`, `matchingIds: ["reproProbe__probe_tool"]`, `catalogToolNames: ["probe_tool"]`.
- Warm read: `containsMcpTarget: true`, no stale not-connected notice.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - `git merge-tree --write-tree upstream/main HEAD` → exit 0
  - `git diff --exit-code $(git merge-base upstream/main HEAD)..HEAD -- CHANGELOG.md` → no contributor-authored changelog entry remains
  - `pnpm check:test-types` → passed
  - `pnpm lint --threads=8` → passed
  - `pnpm protocol:check` → passed
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/tools-effective.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-tools.request-boundary.test.ts packages/sdk/src/index.test.ts` → 9 files / 110 tests passed
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts packages/sdk/src/index.e2e.test.ts` → 1 file passed; 4 passed / 1 skipped
  - `cd ui && pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts src/ui/controllers/agents.test.ts src/ui/views/agents-panels-tools-skills.browser.test.ts src/ui/views/agents.test.ts` → 3 files / 28 tests passed
  - real Gateway RPC proof with real stdio MCP server verifies cold read / refresh / warm read behavior
  - local Control UI browser proof at head `b8bcb372b3d08b2b193d894b67d486cd9fc376fc` verifies cold configured-MCP notice/no `Probe Tool`, explicit `tools.effective.refresh`, then `Probe Tool` visible as an MCP tool
- Edge cases checked:
  - final effective policy still filters materialized MCP tools
  - stale MCP config fingerprints produce refresh-needed behavior instead of serving stale MCP tools
  - read path does not call `getOrCreateSessionMcpRuntime()` on cold load
  - UI notice rendering and explicit refresh action
  - SDK helper calls `tools.effective.refresh`
- What you did **not** verify:
  - reporter's exact Flask SSE MCP server / v2026.5.2 setup
  - Tool Search / Code Mode raw provider-body behavior (out of scope)
  - #80909 raw provider-body behavior (out of scope)
  - full cross-platform/browser E2E

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: users may expect MCP tools to appear immediately on panel load.
  - Mitigation: the panel now displays MCP notices and an explicit refresh action; warm catalogs still appear in read-only inventory.
- Risk: refresh can start configured MCP transports.
  - Mitigation: refresh is explicit and registered as `operator.write`; cold `operator.read` inventory no longer starts or lists MCP transports.
- Risk: dashboard/policy projection may still differ from a live inbound-message run when the UI lacks sender identity.
  - Mitigation: do not accept caller-supplied auth/delivery context; use server-derived session fields and final policy only.
- Risk: overlap with #70876.
  - Mitigation: this PR is narrower and avoids unrelated auto-reply/install-package-dir changes; related work is explicitly linked.

target.title: AI-assisted: recover message-tool-only replies written as private finals
target.body:
## Summary
- Retry once when a `message_tool_only` source reply is written as private final text instead of being sent with the message tool.
- If the retry still does not produce a current source reply, return a small visible runtime error instead of letting the turn look like a silent success.
- Tighten the source-reply prompt wording so final-only answers are called out as private in this mode.

## Linked context
I hit this on a Telegram direct Codex app-server turn on v2026.5.26. The run was correctly in `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"`, but the assistant answered only in final text. That final answer stayed private, so the source chat saw nothing and the turn looked stuck even though the model had produced an answer.

This PR keeps the message tool as the delivery path. It does not auto-publish the private final answer; it gives the agent one chance to send the reply through the intended tool, then fails visibly if it still does not produce a current source reply.

## Review follow-up
- Tightened the recovery predicate so a routed/cross-session `message(action=send)` no longer counts as delivery to the current source conversation. The guard now requires `messagingToolSourceReplyPayloads`, matching the existing default-route/source-reply evidence path.
- Moved the recovery check behind the existing planning-only retry path, so plan-only text like “I'll inspect the files now” still gets the normal act-now retry instead of being steered to send a message immediately.
- Added regressions for both cases.

## Relationship to #84485
This complements #84485 rather than replacing it.

#84485 is Discord-specific and explores a source-delivery guard around `runReplyAgent` behavior. This PR stays in the embedded-agent runner and keeps the strict `message_tool_only` privacy boundary: ordinary final text remains private and is not republished as the source reply. The only visible fallback here is a runtime error payload after the model has already had one replay-safe chance to use `message(action=send)` for the current source conversation.

## Behavior proof
The runner regressions cover the failure mode directly:
- first attempt writes ordinary assistant/final text with no source-reply message-tool evidence;
- the runner retries once with a message-tool-only delivery steer;
- a successful retry with default-route source reply evidence ends without duplicate final payloads;
- an exhausted retry returns a visible runtime error payload;
- explicit-route/cross-session message sends do not mask the missing current source reply;
- planning-only text still follows the planning-only retry path first.

Live transport proof for the PR head is still pending. I requested Mantis Telegram proof after pushing the review-fix commit so the PR can get real source-channel evidence without exposing private setup details.

## Tests and validation
- `corepack pnpm format:check -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm test:changed -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm prompt:snapshots:check`
- `CI=1 corepack pnpm check:changed -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`

I did not run the full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` suite locally.

## Risk checklist
- [x] No secrets, private IDs, logs, or local config included.
- [x] Keeps successful current-source `message(action=send)` delivery as the normal path.
- [x] Does not treat explicit-route/cross-session sends as current source replies.
- [x] Does not turn private final text into an automatic source-channel message.
- [x] Preserves planning-only retries before message-tool-only recovery.
- [x] Fails visibly after one retry instead of reporting a quiet terminal success.
- [x] I’m okay with maintainers editing this branch.

## Current review state
AI-assisted change. I traced the delivery path, wrote the patch, addressed the automated review feedback, and ran the validation above locally.

target.title: fix(cron): guard task liveness during startup
target.body:
## Summary

Task registry maintenance now treats cron active-job liveness as non-authoritative until cron startup reconciliation exits. This prevents a pre-start maintenance sweep from marking persisted cron task records lost while the process-local active-job set is still empty after restart.

The change keeps the boundary narrow:

- `activeJobIds` starts non-authoritative after process boot
- `cron.start()` marks liveness reconciling at entry and authoritative in `finally`
- task maintenance preserves only mapped cron tasks while cron liveness is non-authoritative
- once cron start exits, missing active-job state is trusted again

This is AI-assisted.

## Proof

Real setup: local checkout of `openclaw/openclaw`, branch rebased onto `origin/main` at `435e1a74fa`.

Commands run after the rebase/conflict resolution:

```bash
git diff --check
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
pnpm exec vitest run src/cron/service/ops.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts
pnpm exec vitest run src/tasks/task-registry.maintenance.issue-60299.test.ts src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts
```

Observed result:

- `git diff --check`: passed
- core tsgo: passed
- `src/cron/service/ops.test.ts`: 13 passed
- `src/tasks/task-registry.maintenance.issue-60299.test.ts`: 21 passed
- `src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts`: 63 passed

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Task registry maintenance should preserve mapped stale cron task records while cron active-job liveness is non-authoritative during startup, then resume marking missing mapped cron tasks `lost` after cron startup exits and liveness is authoritative.

**Real setup tested:** Local `openclaw/openclaw` checkout on the PR branch, using an isolated temporary state root under `/tmp/openclaw-pr-80707-proof-*` and the repository's TypeScript runtime via `node --import tsx`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node --import tsx .artifacts/pr-80707-real-proof.mjs
```

The script seeds 10-minute-stale mapped cron task records with `runtime: cron` and `sourceId`, runs task maintenance once while cron active-job liveness is non-authoritative, then runs task maintenance again after liveness is marked authoritative.

**Evidence after fix:** Redacted terminal output from the isolated local state-dir run:

```json
{
  "stateRoot": "/tmp/openclaw-pr-80707-proof-s9f07s",
  "scenario": "mapped stale cron task maintenance across cron startup liveness authority",
  "staleAgeMs": 600000,
  "beforeStartupExit": {
    "livenessAuthoritative": false,
    "maintenance": {
      "reconciled": 0,
      "recovered": 0,
      "cleanupStamped": 0,
      "pruned": 0
    },
    "task": {
      "runtime": "cron",
      "sourceId": "proof-cron-before-startup-exit",
      "status": "running",
      "endedAtPresent": false
    }
  },
  "afterStartupExit": {
    "livenessAuthoritative": true,
    "maintenance": {
      "reconciled": 2,
      "recovered": 0,
      "cleanupStamped": 0,
      "pruned": 0
    },
    "task": {
      "runtime": "cron",
      "sourceId": "proof-cron-after-startup-exit",
      "status": "lost",
      "endedAtPresent": true,
      "error": "backing session missing"
    }
  }
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** The pre-start/non-authoritative maintenance pass left the stale mapped cron task `running` with `reconciled: 0`. The post-start/authoritative maintenance pass marked the stale mapped cron task `lost` with `error: "backing session missing"`.

**Not tested:** I did not run the full monorepo `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` locally.

This shows the fixed behavior: task maintenance does not mark a mapped stale cron task lost during the pre-start/non-authoritative window, and does mark mapped stale cron tasks lost after startup liveness becomes authoritative again.

Also ran a focused Codex review against the rebased diff; it reported no blocker. I did not run the full monorepo `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` locally.

target.title: refactor(openai): centralize Codex OAuth flow
target.body:
## Summary

- Refactor OpenAI Codex OAuth to remove duplicated browser/token flow code; no user-visible OAuth behavior change is intended.
- Keep the existing OpenAI plugin OAuth runtime as the single implementation used by plugin auth and legacy compatibility wrappers.
- Preserve legacy `src/llm` login/refresh behavior, including abort propagation, immediate manual-code input, callback handling, credential refresh shape, provider-hook-unavailable fallback, and disabled-plugin failure behavior.
- Provenance: duplicated OpenAI Codex OAuth flow was introduced by `bb46b79d3c14` (`refactor: internalize OpenClaw agent runtime`, #85341).

## Compatibility Review

- Deprecated `src/llm` OpenAI Codex OAuth login/refresh entry points are preserved and covered by focused compatibility tests. The login wrapper still reports browser auth through `onAuth`, still routes fallback prompts through `onPrompt`, still starts `onManualCodeInput` in parallel with browser auth when supplied, and passes the caller `signal` into the provider-owned OAuth hook.
- If the provider runtime hook is temporarily unavailable, legacy login/refresh fall back to the OpenAI plugin public facade. The fallback uses the activated facade loader, so operator plugin disablement still fails closed instead of bypassing plugin activation policy.
- No plugin SDK `ProviderAuthContext` fields are added. Legacy-only cancellation and manual-code hooks are carried through a private OpenAI Codex bridge context, so external provider auth consumers do not get a new API surface.
- Current-main token behavior remains plugin-owned: bounded token request timeout, SSRF-guarded fetch, cancellation, TLS/proxy preflight, callback handling, proxy dispatcher setup, and error rewriting stay in `extensions/openai/openai-codex-oauth*.runtime.ts` / `extensions/openai/openai-codex-provider.runtime.ts` instead of being copied back into core.
- Live OpenAI browser OAuth with real credentials was run after the final branch update in an isolated temp OpenClaw home. The login completed through the local callback flow and saved an `openai-codex` OAuth profile with an `expiresAt` value. Raw auth URLs, temp paths, email/profile id, and credential material are intentionally omitted.

## Live Fix Proof Repro Matrix

Remote proof was rerun on pre-rebase head `f2829ef7f4` with the same patch content through Crabbox using delegated Blacksmith Testbox. The branch was then rebased cleanly onto `origin/main`; current head is `edf12deb57`.

- Provider: `blacksmith-testbox` via Crabbox
- Testbox id: `tbx_01kspehjhah22npkb47p5g5tkg`
- Actions run: `https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26555293890`
- Result: exit 0

Matrix covered:

| Phase | Proof |
|---|---|
| `oauth-runtime` | `extensions/openai/openai-codex-oauth-flow.runtime.test.ts` passed: callback, manual-code, token exchange/refresh, timeout, cancellation coverage |
| `openai-provider` | `extensions/openai/openai-codex-provider.test.ts`, `extensions/openai/provider-auth.contract.test.ts`, and `extensions/openai/index.test.ts` passed, including proxy dispatcher setup before Codex OAuth refresh |
| `legacy-compat` | `src/llm/utils/oauth/openai-codex.test.ts` passed: legacy callback routing, immediate manual-code input, cancellation propagation, refresh shape, provider-runtime-unavailable fallback, and disabled-plugin failure path |
| `provider-bridge` | `src/plugins/provider-openai-codex-oauth.test.ts` passed: bridge delegates to OpenAI provider auth hook, falls back through the activated OpenAI plugin facade when the hook is unavailable, and preserves disabled-plugin failure behavior |
| `refresh-fallback` | `src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth.openai-codex-refresh-fallback.test.ts` passed in both agents-core and agents-support projects |
| `lint` | Focused `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ...` and `git diff --check` passed |
| `build` | `pnpm build` passed on remote Linux/Testbox |

Credentialed OpenAI browser OAuth was also run locally against pre-rebase head `f2829ef` with the same patch content using an isolated temporary `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_HOME`, and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`. The browser login completed through the localhost callback path and `models auth list --provider openai-codex --json` showed one redacted `openai-codex` OAuth profile with `expiresAt` present. The remote matrix above still covers mocked refresh/fallback/proxy/activation paths on Linux.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/openai/openai-codex-oauth-flow.runtime.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenAI Codex OAuth implementation duplication between the bundled OpenAI plugin and legacy core OAuth helpers; no user-visible OAuth behavior change is intended.
Real environment tested: Local macOS worktree with installed repo dependencies and live OpenAI browser OAuth, plus remote Linux Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox.

The duplicated core browser/token implementation is removed without adding exported provider-auth context fields.
What was not tested: A real token refresh after expiry and a real OpenAI Codex model request were not run; refresh/fallback/proxy paths are covered by focused tests and Testbox proof.

target.title: AI-assisted: recover message-tool-only replies written as private finals
target.body:
## Summary
- Retry once when a `message_tool_only` source reply is written as private final text instead of being sent with the message tool.
- If the retry still does not produce a current source reply, return a small visible runtime error instead of letting the turn look like a silent success.
- Tighten the source-reply prompt wording so final-only answers are called out as private in this mode.

## Linked context
I hit this on a Telegram direct Codex app-server turn on v2026.5.26. The run was correctly in `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"`, but the assistant answered only in final text. That final answer stayed private, so the source chat saw nothing and the turn looked stuck even though the model had produced an answer.

This PR keeps the message tool as the delivery path. It does not auto-publish the private final answer; it gives the agent one chance to send the reply through the intended tool, then fails visibly if it still does not produce a current source reply.

## Review follow-up
- Tightened the recovery predicate so a routed/cross-session `message(action=send)` no longer counts as delivery to the current source conversation. The guard now requires `messagingToolSourceReplyPayloads`, matching the existing default-route/source-reply evidence path.
- Moved the recovery check behind the existing planning-only retry path, so plan-only text like “I'll inspect the files now” still gets the normal act-now retry instead of being steered to send a message immediately.
- Added regressions for both cases.

## Relationship to #84485
This complements #84485 rather than replacing it.

#84485 is Discord-specific and explores a source-delivery guard around `runReplyAgent` behavior. This PR stays in the embedded-agent runner and keeps the strict `message_tool_only` privacy boundary: ordinary final text remains private and is not republished as the source reply. The only visible fallback here is a runtime error payload after the model has already had one replay-safe chance to use `message(action=send)` for the current source conversation.

## Behavior proof
The runner regressions cover the failure mode directly:
- first attempt writes ordinary assistant/final text with no source-reply message-tool evidence;
- the runner retries once with a message-tool-only delivery steer;
- a successful retry with default-route source reply evidence ends without duplicate final payloads;
- an exhausted retry returns a visible runtime error payload;
- explicit-route/cross-session message sends do not mask the missing current source reply;
- planning-only text still follows the planning-only retry path first.

Live transport proof for the PR head is still pending. I requested Mantis Telegram proof after pushing the review-fix commit so the PR can get real source-channel evidence without exposing private setup details.

## Tests and validation
- `corepack pnpm format:check -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm test:changed -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm prompt:snapshots:check`
- `CI=1 corepack pnpm check:changed -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`

I did not run the full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` suite locally.

## Risk checklist
- [x] No secrets, private IDs, logs, or local config included.
- [x] Keeps successful current-source `message(action=send)` delivery as the normal path.
- [x] Does not treat explicit-route/cross-session sends as current source replies.
- [x] Does not turn private final text into an automatic source-channel message.
- [x] Preserves planning-only retries before message-tool-only recovery.
- [x] Fails visibly after one retry instead of reporting a quiet terminal success.
- [x] I’m okay with maintainers editing this branch.

## Current review state
AI-assisted change. I traced the delivery path, wrote the patch, addressed the automated review feedback, and ran the validation above locally.
